1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"sync"
6	
7		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"
8		ddxgraphql "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql"
9	)
10	
11	// hubFederationProvider adapts a *federationHub to the
12	// ddxgraphql.FederationProvider interface so federationNodes /
13	// federated{Beads,Runs,Projects} resolvers can read live registry state and
14	// drive fan-out against registered spokes.
15	//
16	// The provider applies StatusUpdates from each fan-out result back to the
17	// registry on the same lock the hub uses, so subsequent /federation page
18	// loads (or fan-out queries) observe the propagated transport-level
19	// status (offline) without an out-of-band reconcile loop.
20	type hubFederationProvider struct {
21		server *Server
22		once   sync.Once
23		client *federation.FanOutClient
24	}
25	
26	func newHubFederationProvider(s *Server) *hubFederationProvider {
27		return &hubFederationProvider{server: s}
28	}
29	
30	// fanOutClient is lazy-built so tests that swap s.hub mid-run see a fresh
31	// client wired to the latest hub config.
32	func (p *hubFederationProvider) fanOutClient() *federation.FanOutClient {
33		p.once.Do(func() {
34			c := federation.NewFanOutClient()
35			c.HubDDxVersion = p.server.hub.hubDDxVersion
36			c.HubSchemaVersion = p.server.hub.hubSchemaVer
37			p.client = c
38		})
39		return p.client
40	}
41	
42	// Spokes returns a copy of the current registry's spokes.
43	func (p *hubFederationProvider) Spokes() []federation.SpokeRecord {
44		if p.server.hub == nil {
45			return nil
46		}
47		p.server.hub.mu.Lock()
48		defer p.server.hub.mu.Unlock()
49		out := make([]federation.SpokeRecord, len(p.server.hub.registry.Spokes))
50		copy(out, p.server.hub.registry.Spokes)
51		return out
52	}
53	
54	// FanOut runs req against every registered spoke and applies any returned
55	// StatusUpdates back to the registry (so transport-level offline transitions
56	// surface on the next /federation load).
57	func (p *hubFederationProvider) FanOut(ctx context.Context, req *federation.FanOutRequest) (*federation.FanOutResult, error) {
58		if p.server.hub == nil {
59			return &federation.FanOutResult{}, nil
60		}
61		spokes := p.Spokes()
62		res, err := p.fanOutClient().Execute(ctx, spokes, req)
63		if err != nil || res == nil {
64			return res, err
65		}
66		if len(res.StatusUpdates) > 0 {
67			p.server.hub.mu.Lock()
68			for nodeID, st := range res.StatusUpdates {
69				_ = p.server.hub.registry.SetStatus(nodeID, st)
70			}
71			p.server.persistFederationLocked()
72			p.server.hub.mu.Unlock()
73		}
74		return res, nil
75	}
76	
77	// Compile-time assertion that hubFederationProvider satisfies the resolver
78	// contract.
79	var _ ddxgraphql.FederationProvider = (*hubFederationProvider)(nil)
80

4625	func (s *Server) handleGraphQLQuery(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
4626		// Gate /graphql on the same localhost auth that protects every /api/*
4627		// handler. Without this, operators running `ddx server --addr 0.0.0.0`
4628		// ship an unauthenticated GraphQL endpoint serving bead data, worker
4629		// logs, mutations, and subscriptions. See opus final-gate review of
4630		// ddx-02d6142d and the scope line "isTrusted() is still the gatekeeper;
4631		// no auth bypass introduced".
4632		if !isTrusted(r) {
4633			writeJSON(w, http.StatusForbidden, map[string]string{"error": "graphql is localhost-only"})
4634			return
4635		}
4636		// Create gqlgen server with the DDX GraphQL schema
4637		var fedProvider ddxgraphql.FederationProvider
4638		if s.hub != nil {
4639			fedProvider = newHubFederationProvider(s)
4640		}
4641		gqlServer := handler.New(ddxgraphql.NewExecutableSchema(ddxgraphql.Config{
4642			Resolvers: &ddxgraphql.Resolver{
4643				State:                              s.state,
4644				WorkingDir:                         s.WorkingDir,
4645				Workers:                            s.workers,
4646				BeadBus:                            s.beadHub,
4647				Actions:                            &workerDispatchAdapter{manager: s.workers},
4648				ExecLogs:                           &execLogAdapter{workingDir: s.WorkingDir},
4649				CoordMetrics:                       &coordMetricsAdapter{reg: s.workers.LandCoordinators},
4650				CSRFTokens:                         s.csrfTokens,
4651				OperatorPromptIdempotency:          s.operatorPromptIdempotency,
4652				OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist: s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist,
4653				Federation:                         fedProvider,
4654			},
4655			Directives: ddxgraphql.DirectiveRoot{},
4656		}))
4657		// Plumb the originating *http.Request into the resolver context so
4658		// resolvers (e.g. operatorPromptSubmit) can validate the CSRF header
4659		// and capture the originating identity from headers tsnetMiddleware
4660		// injects (X-Tailscale-User / X-Tailscale-Node).
4661		r = r.WithContext(ddxgraphql.WithHTTPRequest(r.Context(), r))
4662		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
4663		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
4664		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.Websocket{
4665			Upgrader: websocket.Upgrader{
4666				CheckOrigin: func(r *http.Request) bool { return true },
4667			},
4668			KeepAlivePingInterval: 30 * time.Second,
4669		})
4670	
4671		gqlServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)
4672	}

1	package server
2	
3	// B14.8a: Backend chaos / integration suite for federation. Each subtest
4	// exercises one failure scenario end-to-end against the real hub HTTP
5	// routes (via Server.Handler) and real spoke servers (httptest.Server),
6	// asserting both behavior (caller sees partial result, no panic, others
7	// keep working) AND the resulting per-spoke status transitions in the
8	// hub registry.
9	//
10	// Scenarios (per bead AC):
11	//   1. hub crash mid-fan-out      → caller sees partial result, no panic
12	//   2. stale spoke during query   → marked stale, not blocking
13	//   3. duplicate registration     → idempotent (same identity) + 409 (conflicting identity)
14	//   4. newer-version registration → degraded status recorded
15	//   5. slow-spoke timeout         → per-node timeout enforced, others succeed
16	//   6. hub restart + immediate    → registry rebuilt from disk + repopulated by next heartbeat
17	//
18	// All scenarios are deterministic: no time.Sleep gates correctness; only
19	// per-node deadlines (which fire in milliseconds) and explicit status
20	// reconcile calls drive transitions.
21	
22	import (
23		"context"
24		"encoding/json"
25		"net/http"
26		"net/http/httptest"
27		"os"
28		"path/filepath"
29		"testing"
30		"time"
31	
32		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"
33	)
34	
35	// chaosSpoke pairs an httptest.Server with the SpokeRecord that the hub
36	// would have registered for it, so each scenario can either drive
37	// register-via-HTTP or seed the registry directly.
38	type chaosSpoke struct {
39		srv *httptest.Server
40		rec federation.SpokeRecord
41	}
42	
43	func newChaosSpoke(t *testing.T, nodeID string, handler http.HandlerFunc) *chaosSpoke {
44		t.Helper()
45		mux := http.NewServeMux()
46		mux.HandleFunc("/graphql", handler)
47		srv := httptest.NewServer(mux)
48		t.Cleanup(srv.Close)
49		return &chaosSpoke{
50			srv: srv,
51			rec: federation.SpokeRecord{
52				NodeID:        nodeID,
53				Name:          nodeID,
54				URL:           srv.URL,
55				DDxVersion:    "0.10.5",
56				SchemaVersion: federation.CurrentSchemaVersion,
57				Capabilities:  []string{"beads", "runs"},
58				Status:        federation.StatusActive,
59			},
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// registerVia drives a real /api/federation/register call so we exercise
64	// the full HTTP path (handshake, persistence, status assignment).
65	func registerVia(t *testing.T, s *Server, sp *chaosSpoke, identityFP string) *http.Response {
66		t.Helper()
67		body := map[string]any{
68			"node_id":              sp.rec.NodeID,
69			"identity_fingerprint": identityFP,
70			"name":                 sp.rec.Name,
71			"url":                  sp.rec.URL,
72			"ddx_version":          sp.rec.DDxVersion,
73			"schema_version":       sp.rec.SchemaVersion,
74			"capabilities":         sp.rec.Capabilities,
75		}
76		return federationDoRequest(t, s, "POST", "/api/federation/register", body, "loopback")
77	}
78	
79	// statusOf reads the current hub-side registry status for one spoke.
80	func statusOf(t *testing.T, s *Server, nodeID string) federation.SpokeStatus {
81		t.Helper()
82		snap := s.HubFederationRegistrySnapshot()
83		if snap == nil {
84			t.Fatalf("registry snapshot nil (hub mode not enabled?)")
85		}
86		if rec := snap.FindSpoke(nodeID); rec != nil {
87			return rec.Status
88		}
89		t.Fatalf("spoke %q absent from registry", nodeID)
90		return ""
91	}
92	
93	// applyStatusUpdates writes fan-out side-effects back into the hub
94	// registry the way a production caller would.
95	func applyStatusUpdates(t *testing.T, s *Server, res *federation.FanOutResult) {
96		t.Helper()
97		s.hub.mu.Lock()
98		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
99		for nodeID, st := range res.StatusUpdates {
100			if err := s.hub.registry.SetStatus(nodeID, st); err != nil {
101				t.Fatalf("apply status update %s=%s: %v", nodeID, st, err)
102			}
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// newFanOutClient builds a client tuned for tests: short per-node timeout
107	// so slow-spoke scenarios complete in milliseconds, low concurrency so
108	// goroutine scheduling stays predictable, and version pinned to match
109	// newHubServer.
110	func newChaosFanOutClient(perNode time.Duration) *federation.FanOutClient {
111		c := federation.NewFanOutClient()
112		c.HubDDxVersion = "0.10.5"
113		c.HubSchemaVersion = federation.CurrentSchemaVersion
114		c.PerNodeTimeout = perNode
115		c.MaxConcurrency = 4
116		return c
117	}
118	
119	// TestFederationChaosSuite groups the six chaos/integration scenarios so
120	// they share the suite-level setup without losing per-scenario isolation.

1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"net/http"
8		"strings"
9		"sync"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/federation"
13	)
14	
15	// HubDefaultDDxVersion is the ddx_version the hub advertises when validating
16	// spoke handshakes. It is exposed as a field so tests and operators can pin
17	// the hub's view of "current ddx version" without rebuilding the binary.
18	const HubDefaultDDxVersion = "0.1.0"
19	
20	// federationHub holds the per-server state for hub-mode federation: the
21	// in-memory registry, the disk path used to persist it, the warning logger
22	// (for plain-HTTP accepted registrations), and configuration knobs.
23	type federationHub struct {
24		mu             sync.Mutex
25		registry       *federation.FederationRegistry
26		statePath      string // empty disables disk persistence
27		allowPlainHTTP bool
28		hubDDxVersion  string
29		hubSchemaVer   string
30		warnLog        func(format string, args ...any)
31		warnings       []string // captured plain-HTTP warnings (test introspection)
32		conflictsLog   []string // captured 409 conflict log lines (test introspection)
33		now            func() time.Time
34	}
35	
36	// EnableHubMode mounts the /api/federation/* routes on this server, switches
37	// the hub-mode flag on, and (best effort) loads the registry from disk so a
38	// hub restart can rebuild state from a prior session.
39	//
40	// Must be called before ListenAndServe / ListenAndServeTLS. Calling it more
41	// than once is a no-op after the first call.
42	//
43	// allowPlainHTTP relaxes the default ts-net/loopback peer policy on the
44	// federation routes — non-trusted peers are accepted, but each accepted
45	// registration emits a WARN log entry. This is the operator opt-out for
46	// federations spanning networks where ts-net is not viable.
47	func (s *Server) EnableHubMode(allowPlainHTTP bool) {
48		if s.hub != nil {
49			return
50		}
51		statePath, err := federation.DefaultStatePath()
52		if err != nil {
53			statePath = ""
54		}
55		var reg *federation.FederationRegistry
56		if statePath != "" {
57			if loaded, lerr := federation.LoadStateFrom(statePath); lerr == nil {
58				reg = loaded
59			}
60		}
61		if reg == nil {
62			reg = federation.NewRegistry()
63		}
64		s.hub = &federationHub{
65			registry:       reg,
66			statePath:      statePath,
67			allowPlainHTTP: allowPlainHTTP,
68			hubDDxVersion:  HubDefaultDDxVersion,
69			hubSchemaVer:   federation.CurrentSchemaVersion,
70			warnLog:        func(format string, args ...any) { log.Printf("WARN: "+format, args...) },
71			now:            time.Now,
72		}
73		s.HubMode = true
74	
75		gate := func(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc { return s.requireFederationTrusted(h) }
76		s.route("POST /api/federation/register", gate(s.handleFederationRegister))
77		s.route("POST /api/federation/heartbeat", gate(s.handleFederationHeartbeat))
78		s.route("GET /api/federation/spokes", gate(s.handleFederationListSpokes))
79		s.route("DELETE /api/federation/spokes/{id}", gate(s.handleFederationDeregister))
80	}
81	
82	// HubFederationRegistrySnapshot returns a copy of the current registry, for
83	// tests and operator introspection.
84	func (s *Server) HubFederationRegistrySnapshot() *federation.FederationRegistry {
85		if s.hub == nil {
86			return nil
87		}
88		s.hub.mu.Lock()
89		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
90		out := federation.NewRegistry()
91		out.SchemaVersion = s.hub.registry.SchemaVersion
92		out.Spokes = append(out.Spokes, s.hub.registry.Spokes...)
93		return out
94	}
95	
96	// HubFederationWarnings returns plain-HTTP-accepted-registration WARN messages
97	// captured since the server started. Test-only helper.
98	func (s *Server) HubFederationWarnings() []string {
99		if s.hub == nil {
100			return nil
101		}
102		s.hub.mu.Lock()
103		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
104		return append([]string(nil), s.hub.warnings...)
105	}
106	
107	// HubFederationConflicts returns 409 conflict log messages captured since the
108	// server started. Test-only helper.
109	func (s *Server) HubFederationConflicts() []string {
110		if s.hub == nil {
111			return nil
112		}
113		s.hub.mu.Lock()
114		defer s.hub.mu.Unlock()
115		return append([]string(nil), s.hub.conflictsLog...)
116	}
117	
118	// requireFederationTrusted enforces the federation-specific peer policy:
119	// loopback / ts-net peers are always accepted; non-trusted peers are accepted
120	// only when --federation-allow-plain-http is set. Distinct from

git commit -m "test(server): wire-path coverage for federationNodes via /graphql [ddx-e2c5180b]

Boots Server.New + EnableHubMode, registers a spoke through the real
/api/federation/register handler, posts a federationNodes query to
/graphql, and asserts the spoke surfaces — proving Resolver.Federation
is bound to the live hub registry. Also covers the hub-mode-off path
(empty list, no error). The pre-existing resolver_federation_test.go
suite injects a stub provider directly and would not catch a
regression where Server.handleGraphQLQuery stops setting
Resolver.Federation."

data.text:
Committed.

**Evidence vs ACs:**
- AC 1 (Federation injection wired only when hub != nil): already satisfied at `cli/internal/server/server.go:4637-4640,4653`.
- AC 2 (Adapter implementing FederationProvider, Spokes copy, FanOut applies StatusUpdates under lock + persists): already satisfied at `cli/internal/server/federation_provider.go:43-79`.
- AC 3 (integration test that posts federationNodes and asserts spoke surfaces): added in `cli/internal/server/federation_provider_test.go` (`TestFederationGraphQLWiringSurfacesRegisteredSpoke` + hub-mode-off counter-test).
- AC 4 (`go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/...` green): verified — all packages pass; pre-commit hook also re-ran go-test green.
